nodes.name: Singleton
nodes.essence: Exactly one instance, globally accessible

nodes.name: Factory Method
nodes.essence: Subclasses decide which class to instantiate

nodes.name: Abstract Factory
nodes.essence: Families of related objects behind one interface

nodes.name: Builder
nodes.essence: Construct a complex object step by step

nodes.name: Prototype
nodes.essence: Clone an existing object instead of building new

nodes.name: Adapter
nodes.essence: Makes incompatible interfaces work together

nodes.name: Bridge
nodes.essence: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation

nodes.name: Composite
nodes.essence: Tree structures — treat parts and wholes alike

nodes.name: Decorator
nodes.essence: Attaches behavior without changing the class

nodes.name: Facade
nodes.essence: One simple interface over a complex subsystem

nodes.name: Flyweight
nodes.essence: Shares state to support huge numbers of objects

nodes.name: Proxy
nodes.essence: Stand-in that controls access to another object

nodes.name: Chain of Responsibility
nodes.essence: Passes a request along a chain of handlers

nodes.name: Command
nodes.essence: Turns a request into a standalone object

nodes.name: Interpreter
nodes.essence: Represents grammar rules as a class hierarchy

nodes.name: Iterator
nodes.essence: Traverses a collection without exposing its structure

nodes.name: Mediator
nodes.essence: Centralizes how a set of objects interact

nodes.name: Memento
nodes.essence: Captures and restores an object's internal state

nodes.name: Observer
nodes.essence: Notifies dependents automatically on state change

nodes.name: State
nodes.essence: Behavior changes as an object's internal state changes

nodes.name: Strategy
nodes.essence: Swaps an algorithm's implementation at runtime

nodes.name: Template Method
nodes.essence: Fixes the outline, lets subclasses fill in steps

nodes.name: Visitor
nodes.essence: Adds operations without changing the classes visited

nodes.name: Dependency Injection
nodes.essence: Dependencies are handed in, never built inside

nodes.name: Null Object
nodes.essence: A do-nothing stand-in instead of a null check

nodes.name: Object Pool
nodes.essence: Reuses a fixed set of expensive objects

nodes.name: Service Locator
nodes.essence: A registry that hands back shared services on request

nodes.name: Lazy Initialization
nodes.essence: Defers creating something until it's first needed

nodes.name: Repository
nodes.essence: Collection-like interface over data storage

nodes.name: Unit of Work
nodes.essence: Tracks changes, commits them as one transaction

nodes.name: Data Mapper
nodes.essence: Moves data between objects and a database, decoupled

nodes.name: Active Record
nodes.essence: An object wraps a row and its own persistence

nodes.name: Service Layer
nodes.essence: Defines an application's operations as one boundary

nodes.name: DTO
nodes.essence: Flat object for carrying data across a boundary

nodes.name: Gateway
nodes.essence: One-object wrapper around an external system's API

nodes.name: Transaction Script
nodes.essence: One procedure per business transaction

nodes.name: Layered / N-Tier
nodes.essence: Presentation, logic, and data as separate tiers

nodes.name: MVC
nodes.essence: Model, view, and controller kept independent

nodes.name: MVP
nodes.essence: A presenter mediates between passive view and model

nodes.name: MVVM
nodes.essence: View binds declaratively to a view-model

nodes.name: Hexagonal
nodes.essence: Core domain isolated behind ports and adapters

nodes.name: Event-Driven Architecture
nodes.essence: Components react to events, not direct calls

nodes.name: Microkernel / Plugin
nodes.essence: Minimal core, features loaded as plug-ins

nodes.name: CQRS
nodes.essence: Separate models for reading and for writing

nodes.name: Event Sourcing
nodes.essence: State is a replayable log of events, not a snapshot

nodes.name: Pipe-and-Filter
nodes.essence: Data flows through independent processing stages

nodes.name: Circuit Breaker
nodes.essence: Stops calling a service that's already failing

nodes.name: Retry with Backoff
nodes.essence: Retries transient failures with a growing delay

nodes.name: Bulkhead
nodes.essence: Isolates resources so one failure can't sink all

nodes.name: Health Endpoint Monitoring
nodes.essence: Exposes a route that reports whether a service is healthy

nodes.name: Compensating Transaction
nodes.essence: Undoes completed steps when a later step fails

nodes.name: Queue-Based Load Leveling
nodes.essence: A queue absorbs bursts so consumers work at their own pace

nodes.name: Rate Limiter
nodes.essence: Caps how many requests a client can make in a window

nodes.name: Timeout / Deadline
nodes.essence: Caps how long a call may take before giving up

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.essence: Single entry point that routes to backing services

nodes.name: Backend-for-Frontend
nodes.essence: One backend tailored to each client type

nodes.name: Ambassador
nodes.essence: A proxy that handles networking concerns for a service

nodes.name: Sidecar
nodes.essence: Attaches a helper process alongside the main one

nodes.name: Load Balancer
nodes.essence: Spreads requests across many identical instances

nodes.name: Consistent Hashing
nodes.essence: Distributes keys so adding a node reshuffles the least

nodes.name: Sharding
nodes.essence: Splits data across nodes by a partition key

nodes.name: Gatekeeper
nodes.essence: A dedicated broker validates requests before they reach the service

nodes.name: Valet Key
nodes.essence: A token grants temporary, limited access to a resource directly

nodes.name: CDN
nodes.essence: Caches and serves static content from the edge, near the user

nodes.name: Autoscaling
nodes.essence: Adds or removes instances automatically as load changes

nodes.name: Saga
nodes.essence: Coordinates a multi-step transaction across services

nodes.name: Strangler Fig
nodes.essence: Replaces a legacy system one slice at a time

nodes.name: Outbox
nodes.essence: Writes the event and the state change in one local transaction

nodes.name: Materialized View
nodes.essence: Precomputes a query's result so reads don't recompute it

nodes.name: Leader Election
nodes.essence: Exactly one node coordinates, and the group agrees who

nodes.name: Quorum & Consensus
nodes.essence: A majority of nodes must agree before a write counts

nodes.name: Gossip Protocol
nodes.essence: Nodes spread state by talking to a few random peers

nodes.name: Write-Ahead Log
nodes.essence: Records the intent durably before applying the change

nodes.name: Bloom Filter
nodes.essence: Probabilistic check: definitely absent, or maybe present

nodes.name: Federated Identity
nodes.essence: One identity provider, trusted across many services

nodes.name: Replication
nodes.essence: Keeps copies of data on multiple nodes for durability and reads

nodes.name: Producer-Consumer
nodes.essence: Decouples generating work from processing it

nodes.name: Thread Pool
nodes.essence: Reuses a fixed set of worker threads

nodes.name: Future / Promise
nodes.essence: A placeholder for a result that isn't ready yet

nodes.name: Actor Model
nodes.essence: Isolated actors that only communicate via messages

nodes.name: Reactor
nodes.essence: One loop dispatches events to their handlers

nodes.name: Monitor Object
nodes.essence: Synchronizes access to an object's own methods

nodes.name: Read-Write Lock
nodes.essence: Many readers, or one writer — never both

nodes.name: Backpressure
nodes.essence: A slow consumer signals upstream to slow down

nodes.name: Publish-Subscribe
nodes.essence: Publishers and subscribers never know about each other

nodes.name: Message Queue
nodes.essence: Point-to-point channel, each message consumed once

nodes.name: Message Router
nodes.essence: Directs a message to the right channel based on rules

nodes.name: Content-Based Router
nodes.essence: Routes a message by inspecting its actual content

nodes.name: Message Translator
nodes.essence: Converts a message between two different formats

nodes.name: Splitter
nodes.essence: Breaks one message into many for individual processing

nodes.name: Aggregator
nodes.essence: Combines many related messages back into one

nodes.name: Competing Consumers
nodes.essence: Many consumers pull from one channel to share load

nodes.name: Dead Letter Channel
nodes.essence: Undeliverable messages land somewhere visible, not lost

nodes.name: Correlation Identifier
nodes.essence: Tags related messages so replies can be matched up

nodes.name: Scatter-Gather
nodes.essence: Broadcasts a request, then merges the responses

nodes.name: Wire Tap
nodes.essence: Copies messages to a side channel for inspection, unchanged

nodes.name: Claim Check
nodes.essence: Sends a reference to large data, not the data itself

nodes.name: Idempotency
nodes.essence: The same request applied twice has the same effect as once

nodes.name: Cache-Aside
nodes.essence: The app checks the cache first, fills it on a miss

nodes.name: Read-Through
nodes.essence: The cache loads on a miss, the app never sees the gap

nodes.name: Write-Through
nodes.essence: Writes hit the cache and the store together, synchronously

nodes.name: Write-Behind
nodes.essence: Writes hit the cache now, the store catches up later

nodes.name: Refresh-Ahead
nodes.essence: Proactively refreshes hot entries before they expire

nodes.name: Entity
nodes.essence: Defined by identity, not by its attributes

nodes.name: Value Object
nodes.essence: Defined only by its attributes, no identity

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.essence: A cluster of objects treated as one consistency unit

nodes.name: Domain Event
nodes.essence: Something meaningful that happened in the domain

nodes.name: Bounded Context
nodes.essence: A model's boundary of validity and meaning

nodes.name: Anti-Corruption Layer
nodes.essence: Translates between two different domain models

nodes.name: Functor
nodes.essence: A type you can map a function over

nodes.name: Monad
nodes.essence: Sequences computations that carry extra context

nodes.name: Pipeline / Composition
nodes.essence: Builds behavior by composing small functions

nodes.name: Immutability
nodes.essence: Never mutate — always return new data

nodes.name: Lens / Optics
nodes.essence: A composable getter/setter into nested data

nodes.name: Currying
nodes.essence: Pre-fill some arguments, get back a function

nodes.name: Dummy Object
nodes.essence: Passed in to satisfy a signature, never actually used

nodes.name: Test Stub
nodes.essence: Returns canned answers, doesn't care how it's called

nodes.name: Test Spy
nodes.essence: Records how it was called so the test can check later

nodes.name: Mock Object
nodes.essence: Pre-programmed with expectations, fails if they're unmet

nodes.name: Fake Object
nodes.essence: A working lightweight stand-in, like an in-memory database

nodes.name: Page Object
nodes.essence: Wraps a UI screen's structure behind one testable interface

nodes.name: Test Data Builder
nodes.essence: Fluent construction of valid objects for test setup

nodes.name: Golden Master
nodes.essence: Compares fresh output against a saved known-good snapshot

nodes.name: Arrange-Act-Assert
nodes.essence: Set up state, perform the action, check the outcome

nodes.name: Authentication Enforcer
nodes.essence: Centralizes verifying who is making the request

nodes.name: Authorization Enforcer (RBAC)
nodes.essence: Centralizes checking what a role is allowed to do

nodes.name: Secure Session Manager
nodes.essence: Issues, tracks, and expires session state safely

nodes.name: Intercepting Validator
nodes.essence: Validates and sanitizes input before it reaches logic

nodes.name: Secure Logger
nodes.essence: Records events for audit without leaking sensitive data

nodes.name: Single Access Point
nodes.essence: One well-guarded entry, instead of many small ones

nodes.name: Least Privilege
nodes.essence: Every component gets the minimum access it needs, no more

nodes.name: God Object
nodes.essence: One class knows and does almost everything

nodes.name: Spaghetti Code
nodes.essence: Control flow too tangled to follow

nodes.name: Big Ball of Mud
nodes.essence: No discernible architecture at all

nodes.name: Anemic Domain Model
nodes.essence: Data objects with no real behavior of their own

nodes.name: Golden Hammer
nodes.essence: One familiar tool applied to every problem

nodes.name: Boat Anchor
nodes.essence: Dead code or hardware kept around unused

nodes.name: CAP Theorem
nodes.essence: When the network splits, you choose consistency or availability — never both

nodes.name: Streaming
nodes.essence: Processing unbounded data as it arrives, not in batches

nodes.name: Handling Spikes
nodes.essence: Absorb, shed, or scale when traffic suddenly surges

nodes.name: Performance
nodes.essence: Serving more, faster: load balancing, caching, and memory

nodes.name: Auth & Access
nodes.essence: Who are you, what may you do, and how is that enforced

nodes.name: Scalability
nodes.essence: Growing capacity with load without rewriting the system

nodes.name: Consistency & Replication
nodes.essence: Keeping copies of data in agreement across nodes

nodes.name: Observability
nodes.essence: Knowing what a running system is actually doing

nodes.name: Resilience
nodes.essence: Staying up, or degrading gracefully, when parts fail
